text: The Biot-Savart law gives the magnetic field due to a current element \(Id\vec{l}\) as:
explanation: Biot-Savart law: \( d\vec{B} = \frac{\mu_0}{4\pi}\frac{Id\vec{l}\times\hat{r}}{r^2} \). Direction given by right-hand rule.
name: Biot-Savart Law

text: The magnetic field at the centre of a circular loop of radius \(R\) carrying current \(I\) is:
explanation: Centre of circular loop: \( B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2R} \). For N turns: \( B = \frac{\mu_0 NI}{2R} \).
name: Field at Centre of Circular Loop

explanation: Ampere's law: \( \oint \vec{B}\cdot d\vec{l} = \mu_0 I_{enc} \). Used to find \(B\) for symmetric current distributions (solenoid, toroid).
name: Ampere's Circuital Law

text: The magnetic field inside a solenoid with \(n\) turns per unit length carrying current \(I\) is:
explanation: Solenoid (infinite): \( B = \mu_0 nI \), uniform inside. Here \(n = N/L\) = turns per unit length. B is nearly zero outside.
name: Field Inside Solenoid

text: The force on a charge \(q\) moving with velocity \(\vec{v}\) in magnetic field \(\vec{B}\) is:
explanation: Lorentz magnetic force: \( \vec{F} = q(\vec{v}\times\vec{B}) \). Magnitude: \( F = qvB\sin\theta \). Always perpendicular to \(\vec{v}\) → no work done.
name: Lorentz Force

text: The radius of circular motion of a charged particle in a magnetic field is:
explanation: Circular motion in \(B\): centripetal force = magnetic force: \( \frac{mv^2}{r} = qvB \Rightarrow r = \frac{mv}{qB} \). Used in mass spectrometer.
name: Radius of Circular Motion in B

text: The force per unit length between two parallel current-carrying conductors is:
explanation: \( F/L = \frac{\mu_0 I_1 I_2}{2\pi d} \). Same direction currents attract; opposite directions repel. This defines the Ampere.
name: Force Between Parallel Conductors

text: The torque on a current loop of area \(A\) with \(N\) turns in field \(B\) is:
explanation: Torque on magnetic dipole: \( \tau = NIA\,B\sin\theta = MB\sin\theta \), where \(M=NIA\) is magnetic moment. Basis of galvanometers.
name: Torque on Current Loop

text: In a moving coil galvanometer, the current is proportional to:
options: Deflection angle: \( I = \frac{C}{NAB}\theta \) (where C = restoring couple constant)
explanation: Galvanometer: \( NIAB = C\theta \Rightarrow I = \frac{C\theta}{NAB} \propto \theta \). Deflection proportional to current.
name: Galvanometer Principle

text: A galvanometer is converted to an ammeter by connecting:
explanation: Ammeter: shunt \(S\) in parallel with galvanometer. \( I_g G = (I-I_g)S \Rightarrow S = \frac{I_g G}{I-I_g} \). Low resistance so most current bypasses galvanometer.
name: Galvanometer to Ammeter

text: A galvanometer is converted to a voltmeter by connecting:
explanation: Voltmeter: high resistance \(R\) in series with galvanometer. \( V = I_g(G + R) \Rightarrow R = V/I_g - G \). High resistance so very little current drawn.
name: Galvanometer to Voltmeter

text: The cyclotron frequency \(f_c\) (independent of speed) is:
explanation: Cyclotron frequency: \( f_c = \frac{qB}{2\pi m} \). Independent of speed (radius increases but period stays same). Maximum KE limited by relativistic effects.
name: Cyclotron Frequency

text: The magnetic field due to a long straight wire at distance \(r\) is:
explanation: Long straight wire: \( B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2\pi r} \) (by Ampere's law with circular path). Circular field lines around wire.
name: Field of Straight Wire

text: The SI unit of magnetic flux density (magnetic field) is:
explanation: Tesla (T) = Wb/m² = kg/(A·s²). Also: 1 T = 10⁴ Gauss. Earth's field ≈ 50 μT.
name: Unit of Magnetic Field

text: The Hall effect is used to determine:
options: Sign of charge carriers and carrier density: \( R_H = 1/(ne) \)
explanation: Hall effect: when current-carrying conductor is in \(\vec{B}\), transverse voltage (Hall voltage) develops. Determines sign of carriers and \( n = 1/(eR_H) \).
name: Hall Effect
